scope: This subject is designed to impart fundamental knowledge on the structure, chemistry and therapeutic value of drugs. The subject emphasis on modern techniques of rational drug design like quantitative structure activity relationship (QSAR), Prodrug concept, combinatorial chemistry and Computer aided drug design (CADD). The subject also emphasizes on the chemistry, mechanism of action, metabolism, adverse effects, Structure Activity Relationships (SAR), therapeutic uses and synthesis of important drugs.
objectives: Understand the importance of drug design and different techniques of drug design.
Understand the chemistry of drugs with respect to their biological activity.
Know the metabolism, adverse effects and therapeutic value of drugs.
Know the importance of SAR of drugs.
courseOutcomes: CO1: Illustrate the history of antibiotics with a detailed study on the nomenclature, stereochemistry, SAR and chemical degradation of beta-lactam, aminoglycosides and tetracycline antibiotics

CO2: Outline the history, nomenclature, stereochemistry, SAR and chemical degradation of macrolide antibiotics. Categorizing the structure, synthesis, mechanism of action of Antimalarial and Explain the concept and applications of Prodrugs

CO3: Classify anti-tubercular agents and outline the chemical structure and synthesis of anti-tubercular agents. Discuss the concept of Urinary tract anti-infective agents and antiviral agents with an emphasis on structure, synthesis, mechanism of action and uses.

CO4: Investigate the fungal, protozoa and helminthic infections and to discuss about the chemical structure and synthesis of anti-fungal, antiprotozoal and anthelmintic agent and categorize the history, development of sulfonamide antibacterial with interpretation on its synthesis.

CO5: Illustrate the technique of Quantitative Structure Activity Relationship (QSAR) through estimation of physicochemical properties such as partition coefficient, hammed substituent constant and Taft steric factor and outline the application of combinatorial chemistry involved in the synthesis of drugs.

scope: This subject is intended to impart the fundamental knowledge on various aspects (classification, mechanism of action, therapeutic effects, clinical uses, side effects and contraindications) of drugs acting on respiratory and gastrointestinal system, infectious diseases, immuno-pharmacology and in addition, emphasis on the principles of toxicology and chronopharmacology.
objectives: understand the mechanism of drug action and its relevance in the treatment of different infectious diseases
comprehend the principles of toxicology and treatment of various poisonings and
appreciate correlation of pharmacology with related medical sciences.
courseOutcomes: CO1: Explain and illustrating the mechanism of drug action of various drugs of the respiratory and GI system along with its side effects, drug interactions for its treatment in GI diseases & Disorders.
CO2: Describe the various chemotherapeutics agents involved in treatment of infectious diseases along with the utilization of antibiotics.

CO3: Ascertain the usage of chemotherapeutic agents for treating fungal, leprosy, viral, malarial infections in addition to explaining and illustrating the mechanism of action of chemotherapeutic drugs with its side effects
CO4: Describe the chemotherapy of cancer and drugs utilized along with summarizing the role of immunotherapy in Pharmacological treatment of cancer
CO5: Summarise the principles of toxicology and different types of poisoning in addition to comprehending the toxic effects of drugs and its management.

scope: This subject gives the student the knowledge of basic understanding of herbal drug industry, the quality of raw material, guidelines for quality of herbal drugs, herbal cosmetics, natural sweeteners, nutraceutical etc. The subject also emphasizes on Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP), patenting and regulatory issues of herbal drugs
objectives: understand raw material as source of herbal drugs from cultivation to herbal drug product
know the WHO and ICH guidelines for evaluation of herbal drugs
know the herbal cosmetics, natural sweeteners, nutraceuticals
appreciate patenting of herbal drugs, GMP.
courseOutcomes: CO1: Explain the role of herbs as raw materials, G.A.P, organic farming and biodynamic agriculture
CO2: Describe basic systems of traditional medicine (Ayurveda, Siddha, Unani, Homeopathy) and standardization of formulations
CO3: Enumerate the market growth, health benefits and herb food interactions of Nutraceuticals
CO4: Explain raw materials and preparation of herbal cosmetics and formulations
CO5: Summarize drug evaluation, patenting and regulatory guidelines including GMP and Schedule T

scope: This subject is designed to impart knowledge and skills necessary for dose calculations, dose adjustments and to apply Biopharmaceutics theories in practical problem solving. Basic theoretical discussions of the principles of Biopharmaceutics and pharmacokinetics are provided to help the students to clarify the concepts.
objectives: Understand the basic concepts in biopharmaceutics and pharmacokinetics.
Use plasma data and derive the pharmacokinetic parameters to describe the process of drug absorption, distribution, metabolism and elimination.
Critically evaluate biopharmaceutic studies involving drug product equivalency
Design and evaluate dosage regimens of the drugs using pharmacokinetic and biopharmaceutic parameters.
detect potential clinical pharmacokinetic problems and apply basic pharmacokinetic principles to solve them
courseOutcomes: CO1: Explain the concept of biopharmaceutics and its applications in product development in relation to Absorption, Distribution and Elimination.
CO2: Evaluate the pharmacokinetic processes and their relevance in efficacy of dosage form in addition to the concepts of bioavailability and bioequivalence studies.
CO3: Distinguish the Pharmacokinetic studies and learning various compartmental models.
CO4: Compare the concept and mechanisms of dissolution, IVIVC correlation and calculation of loading and maintenance doses with their significance in clinical settings.
CO5: Illustrate the nonlinear Pharmacokinetics and biotransformation of drugs.

scope: Biotechnology has a long promise to revolutionize the biological sciences and technology. Scientific application of biotechnology in the field of genetic engineering, medicine and fermentation technology makes the subject interesting. Biotechnology is leading to new biological revolutions in diagnosis, prevention and cure of diseases, new and cheaper pharmaceutical drugs.
objectives: Understanding the importance of Immobilized enzymes in Pharmaceutical Industries
Genetic engineering applications in relation to production of pharmaceuticals
Importance of Monoclonal antibodies in Industries
Appreciate the use of microorganisms in fermentation technology
courseOutcomes: CO1: Explain the basic knowledge on the biotechnology related to pharmacy, enzyme immobilization technique and the concept of biosensors in pharmaceutical industries.
CO2: Summarize the prerequisite of recombinant DNA technology, recombinant technology, polymerase reaction techniques in pharmaceutical product development.
CO3: Explain the anatomy and role of the immune cells, immune responses, immunological products followed by the manufacturing and utilization of monoclonal antibodies.
CO4: Illustrate the immune blotting technique in identification of diseases, mutation, microbial transformation process, and uses in the drug production.
CO5: Evaluate the fermentation process, microbial metabolite, blood products and to compute the fermentation design for the industrial use.

scope: This course deals with the various aspects of quality control and quality assurance aspects of pharmaceutical industries. It covers the important aspects like cGMP, QC tests, documentation, quality certifications and regulatory affairs.
objectives: understand the cGMP aspects in a pharmaceutical industry
appreciate the importance of documentation
understand the scope of quality certifications applicable to pharmaceutical industries
understand the responsibilities of QA and QC departments
courseOutcomes: CO1: Describe cGMP aspects in a pharmaceutical industry.
CO2: Explain the importance of documentation.
CO3: Enumerate the scope of quality certifications applicable to pharmaceutical industries.
CO4: Describe the responsibilities of QA departments.
CO5: Describe the responsibilities of QC departments.

scope: Practical training in the preparation of medicinally important drugs and intermediates, assaying methods, microwave irradiation techniques, and drug design software tools.
objectives: To carry out the preparation of medicinally important compounds
To understand the calculation of molecular weight, practical yield and percentage yield
To learn different types of titrations and carry out assay for medicinally important compounds
To prepare eco-friendly compounds using microwave irradiation
To understand drug design using chemical sketching of compounds and calculating its pharmacokinetic properties using online software tool
courseOutcomes: CO1: Synthesize and assaying drugs of medicinal use such as sulphanilamide, dapsone, metronidazole, hexamine, benzylpenicillin etc.
CO2: Examine microwave irradiation technique in the preparation of medicinally important compounds
CO3: Illustrate and justifying chemical structures using software tools such as chemdraw
CO4: Identify physicochemical properties such as log P, MW, MR using online software tool such as pkcsm and compare and contrast with experimental data
CO5: Synthesis of Medicinally important compounds using Microwave irradiation

units.name: Unit I
units.title: Medicinal Chemistry - III Practicals
units.description: Drug syntheses, assays of key compounds, microwave-assisted synthesis, structures drawing, and Lipinski's Rule of 5 screening.

scope: Practical sessions covering dose calculations, screening models for anti-allergic/anti-ulcer activity, bioassays using guinea pig ileum, acute toxicity testing, and biostatistical analysis.
objectives: To demonstrate the maintenance of laboratory animals, blood collections techniques
To know about the screening methods of anti-ulcer and anti-allergic activity
To Understand about various screening methods for toxicity
To understand isolation of different organs from the laboratory animals by simulated experiments
To know about biostatistics involved in pharmacology
courseOutcomes: CO1: Explain the dose calculations of various drugs used in animal experimentation. Understand and perform the screening models of anti-allergic and anti-ulcer drugs.

CO2: Determine the various biochemical parameters and perform the bioassay of different agonists and antagonists using guinea pig ileum preparation. Understand and perform the screening models of GI drugs
CO3: Evaluate the concepts of acute oral toxicity of various substances. Understand and perform the screening models of anti-diabetic drugs and pyrogen testing
CO4: Explain the of various substances. Understand and calculate various biostatistical methods for use in animal experimentation.

CO5: Explain the concepts of acute eye irritation studies and skin irritation studies of various drugs used in animal experimentation. Understand and perform the screening models of saline purgatives.

scope: Practical experiments for phytochemical screening of crude drugs, excipient evaluation, cosmetic/herbal formulation and evaluation, and monograph analyses.
objectives: To perform preliminary phytochemical screening of crude drugs
To carry out determination of alcohol content and total alkaloids
To formulate and evaluate herbal cosmetics and conventional dosage forms
courseOutcomes: CO1: To perform preliminary phytochemical screening of crude drugs.
CO2: Determination of the alcohol content of Asava and Arista
CO3: Evaluation of excipients of natural origin
CO4: Incorporation of prepared and standardized extract in cosmetic formulations like creams, lotions and shampoos and their evaluation.
CO5: Incorporation of prepared and standardized extract in formulations like syrups, mixtures and tablets and their evaluation as per Pharmacopoeial requirements.
